question: What steps do I need to follow to decrease the quantity of a single product on my phone?

question: What are the steps to create a compound medication in E-Prescribe?
ground_truth: To create a compound medication in E-Prescribe, go to Customers and select a customer. Then, select the patient's profile and go to the E-Prescribe tab. In Prescriptions, select Prescribe New Drug → Compound/Specialty → Setup Compound. Enter a Title and a Comment for the compound, then select Add Compound. Next, select Edit next to Compound to enter Ingredients, Quantity, Directions, and the Pharmacy for the compound medication, then select Update Compound.

1. Go to Customers and select a customer.
2. Select the patient's profile and go to the E-Prescribe tab.
3. In Prescriptions, select Prescribe New Drug → Compound/Specialty → Setup Compound.
4. Enter a Title and a Comment for the compound, then select Add Compound.
5. Next to Compound, select Edit.
6. Enter Ingredients, Quantity, Directions, and select the Pharmacy.
7. Select Update Compound.

Note: You need permission to set up a compound medication, and this feature requires E-Prescribe. Source: https://support.vagaro.com/hc/en-us/articles/41163913325979-Set-Up-and-Prescribe-a-Compound-Medication
justification: The AI's response accurately outlines the steps to create a compound medication in E-Prescribe and includes additional helpful information about permissions and feature requirements.

question: How do I manually configure my Star wireless printer to connect to my Wi-Fi network using an Android device?
ground_truth: To manually configure your Star wireless printer on Android, ensure the printer is OFF, press the FEED button while turning it ON to print network info, then connect your Android device to the printer's Wi-Fi (not internet). Open a browser and go to 192.168.10.1, log in with username 'root' and password 'public', select WLAN Settings, choose Infrastructure Mode, enter your SSID and Security Key, set Security to WPA2/AES, then go to IP Parameters, select Static, and input your IP Address, Subnet Mask, and Default Gateway. Save and execute the settings; the printer will restart and connect to your network.
generated_response:
To manually configure your Star wireless printer on an Android device:

1. Ensure the printer is OFF. Press and hold the FEED button while turning it ON to perform a self-print. Release the button when printing begins.
2. Find the MAC address under "Network Configuration" on the printout.
3. On your Android device, go to Settings > Wi-Fi, and connect to the printer's network (look for the last 6 digits of the MAC address). "Connected, no internet" will appear.
4. Open a browser and go to 192.168.10.1 (or the IP address from the printout).
5. Log in with username "root" and password "public" (do not change the password).
6. Select WLAN Settings > Infrastructure Mode. Enter your Wi-Fi SSID and Security Key (WPA2/AES), then submit.
7. Choose IP Parameters > Static. Enter your IP Address, Subnet Mask, and Default Gateway.
8. Save and execute the settings. The printer will restart and connect to your Wi-Fi network.

Source: https://support.vagaro.com/hc/en-us/articles/115000256554-Star-WIRELESS-Printer-Setup-Android-Devices
justification: The AI's response accurately covers the steps needed to manually configure the Star wireless printer on an Android device, aligning well with the ground truth answer.
